tools.name: instances.deleteInstancesId
tools.description: Delete the DICOM instance whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: instances.deleteInstancesIdAttachmentsName
tools.description: Delete an attachment associated with the given DICOM instance. This call will fail if trying to delete a system attachment (i.e. whose index is < 1024).

tools.name: instances.deleteInstancesIdMetadataName
tools.description: Delete some metadata associated with the given DICOM instance. This call will fail if trying to delete a system metadata (i.e. whose index is < 1024).

tools.name: instances.getInstances
tools.description: List the Orthanc identifiers of all the available DICOM instances

tools.name: instances.getInstancesId
tools.description: Get detailed information about the DICOM instance whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdAttachments
tools.description: Get the list of attachments that are associated with the given instance

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdAttachmentsNameCompressedData
tools.description: Get the (binary) content of one attachment associated with the given instance. The attachment will not be decompressed if `StorageCompression` is `true`.

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdAttachmentsNameCompressedMd5
tools.description: Get the MD5 hash of one attachment associated with the given instance, as stored on the disk. This is different from `.../md5` iff `EnableStorage` is `true`.

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdAttachmentsNameCompressedSize
tools.description: Get the size of one attachment associated with the given instance, as stored on the disk. This is different from `.../size` iff `EnableStorage` is `true`.

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdAttachmentsNameData
tools.description: Get the (binary) content of one attachment associated with the given instance

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdAttachmentsNameInfo
tools.description: Get all the information about the attachment associated with the given instance

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdAttachmentsNameIsCompressed
tools.description: Test whether the attachment has been stored as a compressed file on the disk.

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdAttachmentsNameMd5
tools.description: Get the MD5 hash of one attachment associated with the given instance

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdAttachmentsNameSize
tools.description: Get the size of one attachment associated with the given instance

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdContent
tools.description: Get the raw content of one DICOM tag in the hierarchy of DICOM dataset

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdFile
tools.description: Download one DICOM instance

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdFrames
tools.description: List the frames that are available in the DICOM instance of interest

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdFramesFrameImageInt16
tools.description: Decode one frame of interest from the given DICOM instance. Pixels of grayscale images are truncated to the [-32768,32767] range. Negative values must be interpreted according to two's complement.

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdFramesFrameImageUint16
tools.description: Decode one frame of interest from the given DICOM instance. Pixels of grayscale images are truncated to the [0,65535] range.

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdFramesFrameImageUint8
tools.description: Decode one frame of interest from the given DICOM instance. Pixels of grayscale images are truncated to the [0,255] range.

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdFramesFrameMatlab
tools.description: Decode one frame of interest from the given DICOM instance, and export this frame as a Octave/Matlab matrix to be imported with `eval()`: https://book.orthanc-server.com/faq/matlab.html

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdFramesFrameNumpy
tools.description: Decode one frame of interest from the given DICOM instance, for use with numpy in Python. The numpy array has 3 dimensions: (height, width, color channel).

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdFramesFramePreview
tools.description: Decode one frame of interest from the given DICOM instance. The full dynamic range of grayscale images is rescaled to the [0,255] range.

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdFramesFrameRaw
tools.description: Access the raw content of one individual frame of the DICOM instance of interest, bypassing image decoding. This is notably useful to access the source files in compressed transfer syntaxes.

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdFramesFrameRawGz
tools.description: Access the raw content of one individual frame of the DICOM instance of interest, bypassing image decoding. This is notably useful to access the source files in compressed transfer syntaxes. The image is compressed using gzip

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdFramesFrameRendered
tools.description: Render one frame of interest from the given DICOM instance. This function takes scaling into account (`RescaleSlope` and `RescaleIntercept` tags), as well as the default windowing stored in the DICOM file (`WindowCenter` and `WindowWidth`tags), and can be used to resize the resulting image. Color images are not affected by windowing.

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdHeader
tools.description: Get the DICOM tags in the meta-header of the DICOM instance. By default, the `full` format is used, which combines hexadecimal tags with human-readable description.

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdImageInt16
tools.description: Decode the first frame of the given DICOM instance. Pixels of grayscale images are truncated to the [-32768,32767] range. Negative values must be interpreted according to two's complement.

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdImageUint16
tools.description: Decode the first frame of the given DICOM instance. Pixels of grayscale images are truncated to the [0,65535] range.

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdImageUint8
tools.description: Decode the first frame of the given DICOM instance. Pixels of grayscale images are truncated to the [0,255] range.

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdMatlab
tools.description: Decode the first frame of the given DICOM instance., and export this frame as a Octave/Matlab matrix to be imported with `eval()`: https://book.orthanc-server.com/faq/matlab.html

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdMetadata
tools.description: Get the list of metadata that are associated with the given instance

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdMetadataName
tools.description: Get the value of a metadata that is associated with the given instance

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdModule
tools.description: Get the instance module of the DICOM instance whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdNumpy
tools.description: Decode the given DICOM instance, for use with numpy in Python. The numpy array has 4 dimensions: (frame, height, width, color channel).

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdPatient
tools.description: Get detailed information about the parent patient of the DICOM instance whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdPdf
tools.description: Get the PDF file that is embedded in one DICOM instance. If the DICOM instance doesn't contain the `EncapsulatedDocument` tag or if the `MIMETypeOfEncapsulatedDocument` tag doesn't correspond to the PDF type, a `404` HTTP error is raised.

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdPreview
tools.description: Decode the first frame of the given DICOM instance. The full dynamic range of grayscale images is rescaled to the [0,255] range.

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdRendered
tools.description: Render the first frame of the given DICOM instance. This function takes scaling into account (`RescaleSlope` and `RescaleIntercept` tags), as well as the default windowing stored in the DICOM file (`WindowCenter` and `WindowWidth`tags), and can be used to resize the resulting image. Color images are not affected by windowing.

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdSeries
tools.description: Get detailed information about the parent series of the DICOM instance whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdSimplifiedTags
tools.description: Get the DICOM tags in human-readable format (same as the `/instances/{id}/tags?simplify` route)

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdStatistics
tools.description: Get statistics about the given instance

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdStudy
tools.description: Get detailed information about the parent study of the DICOM instance whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: instances.getInstancesIdTags
tools.description: Get the DICOM tags in the specified format. By default, the `full` format is used, which combines hexadecimal tags with human-readable description.

tools.name: instances.postInstances
tools.description: Upload DICOM instances

tools.name: instances.postInstancesIdAnonymize
tools.description: Download an anonymized version of the DICOM instance whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL: https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/anonymization.html#anonymization-of-a-single-instance

tools.name: instances.postInstancesIdAttachmentsNameCompress
tools.description: Change the compression scheme that is used to store an attachment.

tools.name: instances.postInstancesIdAttachmentsNameUncompress
tools.description: Change the compression scheme that is used to store an attachment.

tools.name: instances.postInstancesIdAttachmentsNameVerifyMd5
tools.description: Verify that the attachment is not corrupted, by validating its MD5 hash

tools.name: instances.postInstancesIdExport
tools.description: Write the DICOM file onto the filesystem where Orthanc is running

tools.name: instances.postInstancesIdModify
tools.description: Download a modified version of the DICOM instance whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL: https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/anonymization.html#modification-of-a-single-instance

tools.name: instances.postInstancesIdReconstruct
tools.description: Reconstruct the main DICOM tags in DB of the instance whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL. This is useful if child studies/series/instances have inconsistent values for higher-level tags, in order to force Orthanc to use the value from the resource of interest. Beware that this is a time-consuming operation, as all the children DICOM instances will be parsed again, and the Orthanc index will be updated accordingly.

tools.name: instances.putInstancesIdAttachmentsName
tools.description: Attach a file to the given DICOM instance. This call will fail if trying to modify a system attachment (i.e. whose index is < 1024).

tools.name: instances.putInstancesIdMetadataName
tools.description: Set the value of some metadata in the given DICOM instance. This call will fail if trying to modify a system metadata (i.e. whose index is < 1024).

tools.name: jobs.getJobs
tools.description: List all the available jobs

tools.name: jobs.getJobsId
tools.description: Retrieve detailed information about the job whose identifier is provided in the URL: https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/advanced-rest.html#jobs

tools.name: jobs.getJobsIdKey
tools.description: Retrieve some output produced by a job. As of Orthanc 1.8.2, only the jobs that generate a DICOMDIR media or a ZIP archive provide such an output (with `key` equals to `archive`).

tools.name: jobs.postJobsIdCancel
tools.description: Cancel the job whose identifier is provided in the URL. Check out the Orthanc Book for more information about the state machine applicable to jobs: https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/advanced-rest.html#jobs

tools.name: jobs.postJobsIdPause
tools.description: Pause the job whose identifier is provided in the URL. Check out the Orthanc Book for more information about the state machine applicable to jobs: https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/advanced-rest.html#jobs

tools.name: jobs.postJobsIdResubmit
tools.description: Resubmit the job whose identifier is provided in the URL. Check out the Orthanc Book for more information about the state machine applicable to jobs: https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/advanced-rest.html#jobs

tools.name: jobs.postJobsIdResume
tools.description: Resume the job whose identifier is provided in the URL. Check out the Orthanc Book for more information about the state machine applicable to jobs: https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/advanced-rest.html#jobs

tools.name: logs.getToolsLogLevel
tools.description: Get the main log level of Orthanc

tools.name: logs.getToolsLogLevelDicom
tools.description: Get the log level of the log category `dicom`

tools.name: logs.getToolsLogLevelGeneric
tools.description: Get the log level of the log category `generic`

tools.name: logs.getToolsLogLevelHttp
tools.description: Get the log level of the log category `http`

tools.name: logs.getToolsLogLevelJobs
tools.description: Get the log level of the log category `jobs`

tools.name: logs.getToolsLogLevelLua
tools.description: Get the log level of the log category `lua`

tools.name: logs.getToolsLogLevelPlugins
tools.description: Get the log level of the log category `plugins`

tools.name: logs.getToolsLogLevelSqlite
tools.description: Get the log level of the log category `sqlite`

tools.name: logs.putToolsLogLevel
tools.description: Set the main log level of Orthanc

tools.name: logs.putToolsLogLevelDicom
tools.description: Set the log level of the log category `dicom`

tools.name: logs.putToolsLogLevelGeneric
tools.description: Set the log level of the log category `generic`

tools.name: logs.putToolsLogLevelHttp
tools.description: Set the log level of the log category `http`

tools.name: logs.putToolsLogLevelJobs
tools.description: Set the log level of the log category `jobs`

tools.name: logs.putToolsLogLevelLua
tools.description: Set the log level of the log category `lua`

tools.name: logs.putToolsLogLevelPlugins
tools.description: Set the log level of the log category `plugins`

tools.name: logs.putToolsLogLevelSqlite
tools.description: Set the log level of the log category `sqlite`

tools.name: networking.deleteModalitiesId
tools.description: Delete one DICOM modality. This change is permanent iff. `DicomModalitiesInDatabase` is `true`, otherwise it is lost at the next restart of Orthanc.

tools.name: networking.deletePeersId
tools.description: Delete one Orthanc peer. This change is permanent iff. `OrthancPeersInDatabase` is `true`, otherwise it is lost at the next restart of Orthanc.

tools.name: networking.deleteQueriesId
tools.description: Delete the query/retrieve operation whose identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: networking.getModalities
tools.description: List all the DICOM modalities that are known to Orthanc. This corresponds either to the content of the `DicomModalities` configuration option, or to the information stored in the database if `DicomModalitiesInDatabase` is `true`.

tools.name: networking.getModalitiesId
tools.description: List the operations that are available for a DICOM modality.

tools.name: networking.getModalitiesIdConfiguration
tools.description: Get detailed information about the configuration of some DICOM modality

tools.name: networking.getPeers
tools.description: List all the Orthanc peers that are known to Orthanc. This corresponds either to the content of the `OrthancPeers` configuration option, or to the information stored in the database if `OrthancPeersInDatabase` is `true`.

tools.name: networking.getPeersId
tools.description: List the operations that are available for an Orthanc peer.

tools.name: networking.getPeersIdConfiguration
tools.description: Get detailed information about the configuration of some Orthanc peer

tools.name: networking.getPeersIdSystem
tools.description: Get system information about some Orthanc peer. This corresponds to doing a `GET` request against the `/system` URI of the remote peer. This route can be used to test connectivity.

tools.name: networking.getQueries
tools.description: List the identifiers of all the query/retrieve operations on DICOM modalities, as initiated by calls to `/modalities/{id}/query`. The length of this list is bounded by the `QueryRetrieveSize` configuration option of Orthanc. https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/rest.html#performing-query-retrieve-c-find-and-find-with-rest

tools.name: networking.getQueriesId
tools.description: List the available operations for the query/retrieve operation whose identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: networking.getQueriesIdAnswers
tools.description: List the indices of all the available answers resulting from a query/retrieve operation on some DICOM modality, whose identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: networking.getQueriesIdAnswersIndex
tools.description: List the available operations on an answer associated with the query/retrieve operation whose identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: networking.getQueriesIdAnswersIndexContent
tools.description: Get the content (DICOM tags) of one answer associated with the query/retrieve operation whose identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: networking.getQueriesIdLevel
tools.description: Get the query level (value of the `QueryRetrieveLevel` tag) of the query/retrieve operation whose identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: networking.getQueriesIdModality
tools.description: Get the identifier of the DICOM modality that was targeted by the query/retrieve operation whose identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: networking.getQueriesIdQuery
tools.description: Get the original DICOM filter associated with the query/retrieve operation whose identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: networking.getStorageCommitmentId
tools.description: Get the storage commitment report whose identifier is provided in the URL: https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/storage-commitment.html#storage-commitment-scu

tools.name: networking.postModalitiesIdEcho
tools.description: Trigger C-ECHO SCU command against the DICOM modality whose identifier is provided in URL: https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/rest.html#performing-c-echo

tools.name: networking.postModalitiesIdFind
tools.description: Trigger a sequence of C-FIND SCU commands against the DICOM modality whose identifier is provided in URL, in order to discover a hierarchy of matching patients/studies/series. Deprecated in favor of `/modalities/{id}/query`.

tools.name: networking.postModalitiesIdFindInstance
tools.description: Trigger C-FIND SCU command against the DICOM modality whose identifier is provided in URL, in order to find an instance. Deprecated in favor of `/modalities/{id}/query`.

tools.name: networking.postModalitiesIdFindPatient
tools.description: Trigger C-FIND SCU command against the DICOM modality whose identifier is provided in URL, in order to find a patient. Deprecated in favor of `/modalities/{id}/query`.

tools.name: networking.postModalitiesIdFindSeries
tools.description: Trigger C-FIND SCU command against the DICOM modality whose identifier is provided in URL, in order to find a series. Deprecated in favor of `/modalities/{id}/query`.

tools.name: networking.postModalitiesIdFindStudy
tools.description: Trigger C-FIND SCU command against the DICOM modality whose identifier is provided in URL, in order to find a study. Deprecated in favor of `/modalities/{id}/query`.

tools.name: networking.postModalitiesIdFindWorklist
tools.description: Trigger C-FIND SCU command against the remote worklists of the DICOM modality whose identifier is provided in URL

tools.name: networking.postModalitiesIdMove
tools.description: Start a C-MOVE SCU command as a job, in order to drive the execution of a sequence of C-STORE commands by some remote DICOM modality whose identifier is provided in the URL: https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/rest.html#performing-c-move

tools.name: networking.postModalitiesIdQuery
tools.description: Trigger C-FIND SCU command against the DICOM modality whose identifier is provided in URL: https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/rest.html#performing-query-retrieve-c-find-and-find-with-rest

tools.name: networking.postModalitiesIdStorageCommitment
tools.description: Trigger a storage commitment request to some remote DICOM modality whose identifier is provided in the URL: https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/storage-commitment.html#storage-commitment-scu

tools.name: networking.postModalitiesIdStore
tools.description: Start a C-STORE SCU command as a job, in order to send DICOM resources stored locally to some remote DICOM modality whose identifier is provided in the URL: https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/rest.html#rest-store-scu

tools.name: networking.postModalitiesIdStoreStraight
tools.description: Synchronously send the DICOM instance in the POST body to the remote DICOM modality whose identifier is provided in URL, without having to first store it locally within Orthanc. This is an alternative to command-line tools such as `storescu` from DCMTK or dcm4che.

tools.name: networking.postPeersIdStore
tools.description: Send DICOM resources stored locally to some remote Orthanc peer whose identifier is provided in the URL: https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/rest.html#sending-one-resource

tools.name: networking.postPeersIdStoreStraight
tools.description: Synchronously send the DICOM instance in the POST body to the Orthanc peer whose identifier is provided in URL, without having to first store it locally within Orthanc. This is an alternative to command-line tools such as `curl`.

tools.name: networking.postQueriesIdAnswersIndexQueryInstances
tools.description: Issue a second DICOM C-FIND operation, in order to query the child instances associated with one answer to some query/retrieve operation whose identifiers are provided in the URL

tools.name: networking.postQueriesIdAnswersIndexQuerySeries
tools.description: Issue a second DICOM C-FIND operation, in order to query the child series associated with one answer to some query/retrieve operation whose identifiers are provided in the URL

tools.name: networking.postQueriesIdAnswersIndexQueryStudies
tools.description: Issue a second DICOM C-FIND operation, in order to query the child studies associated with one answer to some query/retrieve operation whose identifiers are provided in the URL

tools.name: networking.postQueriesIdAnswersIndexRetrieve
tools.description: Start a C-MOVE SCU command as a job, in order to retrieve one answer associated with the query/retrieve operation whose identifiers are provided in the URL: https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/rest.html#performing-retrieve-c-move

tools.name: networking.postQueriesIdRetrieve
tools.description: Start a C-MOVE SCU command as a job, in order to retrieve all the answers associated with the query/retrieve operation whose identifier is provided in the URL: https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/rest.html#performing-retrieve-c-move

tools.name: networking.postStorageCommitmentIdRemove
tools.description: Remove out of Orthanc, the DICOM instances that have been reported to have been properly received the storage commitment report whose identifier is provided in the URL. This is only possible if the `Status` of the storage commitment report is `Success`. https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/storage-commitment.html#removing-the-instances

tools.name: networking.putModalitiesId
tools.description: Define a new DICOM modality, or update an existing one. This change is permanent iff. `DicomModalitiesInDatabase` is `true`, otherwise it is lost at the next restart of Orthanc.

tools.name: networking.putPeersId
tools.description: Define a new Orthanc peer, or update an existing one. This change is permanent iff. `OrthancPeersInDatabase` is `true`, otherwise it is lost at the next restart of Orthanc.

tools.name: other.getInstancesIdAttachmentsName
tools.description: Get the list of the operations that are available for attachments associated with the given instance

tools.name: other.getInstancesIdFramesFrame
tools.description: List the available operations under URI `/instances/{id}/frames/{frame}/`

tools.name: other.getPatientsIdAttachmentsName
tools.description: Get the list of the operations that are available for attachments associated with the given patient

tools.name: other.getSeriesIdAttachmentsName
tools.description: Get the list of the operations that are available for attachments associated with the given series

tools.name: other.getStudiesIdAttachmentsName
tools.description: Get the list of the operations that are available for attachments associated with the given study

tools.name: other.getTools
tools.description: List the available operations under URI `/tools/`

tools.name: patients.deletePatientsId
tools.description: Delete the DICOM patient whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: patients.deletePatientsIdAttachmentsName
tools.description: Delete an attachment associated with the given DICOM patient. This call will fail if trying to delete a system attachment (i.e. whose index is < 1024).

tools.name: patients.deletePatientsIdMetadataName
tools.description: Delete some metadata associated with the given DICOM patient. This call will fail if trying to delete a system metadata (i.e. whose index is < 1024).

tools.name: patients.getPatients
tools.description: List the Orthanc identifiers of all the available DICOM patients

tools.name: patients.getPatientsId
tools.description: Get detailed information about the DICOM patient whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: patients.getPatientsIdArchive
tools.description: Synchronously create a ZIP archive containing the DICOM patient whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL. This flavor is synchronous, which might *not* be desirable to archive large amount of data, as it might lead to network timeouts. Prefer the asynchronous version using `POST` method.

tools.name: patients.getPatientsIdAttachments
tools.description: Get the list of attachments that are associated with the given patient

tools.name: patients.getPatientsIdAttachmentsNameCompressedData
tools.description: Get the (binary) content of one attachment associated with the given patient. The attachment will not be decompressed if `StorageCompression` is `true`.

tools.name: patients.getPatientsIdAttachmentsNameCompressedMd5
tools.description: Get the MD5 hash of one attachment associated with the given patient, as stored on the disk. This is different from `.../md5` iff `EnableStorage` is `true`.

tools.name: patients.getPatientsIdAttachmentsNameCompressedSize
tools.description: Get the size of one attachment associated with the given patient, as stored on the disk. This is different from `.../size` iff `EnableStorage` is `true`.

tools.name: patients.getPatientsIdAttachmentsNameData
tools.description: Get the (binary) content of one attachment associated with the given patient

tools.name: patients.getPatientsIdAttachmentsNameInfo
tools.description: Get all the information about the attachment associated with the given patient

tools.name: patients.getPatientsIdAttachmentsNameIsCompressed
tools.description: Test whether the attachment has been stored as a compressed file on the disk.

tools.name: patients.getPatientsIdAttachmentsNameMd5
tools.description: Get the MD5 hash of one attachment associated with the given patient

tools.name: patients.getPatientsIdAttachmentsNameSize
tools.description: Get the size of one attachment associated with the given patient

tools.name: patients.getPatientsIdInstances
tools.description: Get detailed information about the child instances of the DICOM patient whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: patients.getPatientsIdInstancesTags
tools.description: Get the tags of all the child instances of the DICOM patient whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: patients.getPatientsIdMedia
tools.description: Synchronously create a DICOMDIR media containing the DICOM patient whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL. This flavor is synchronous, which might *not* be desirable to archive large amount of data, as it might lead to network timeouts. Prefer the asynchronous version using `POST` method.

tools.name: patients.getPatientsIdMetadata
tools.description: Get the list of metadata that are associated with the given patient

tools.name: patients.getPatientsIdMetadataName
tools.description: Get the value of a metadata that is associated with the given patient

tools.name: patients.getPatientsIdModule
tools.description: Get the patient module of the DICOM patient whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: patients.getPatientsIdProtected
tools.description: Is the patient protected against recycling?

tools.name: patients.getPatientsIdSeries
tools.description: Get detailed information about the child series of the DICOM patient whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: patients.getPatientsIdSharedTags
tools.description: Extract the DICOM tags whose value is constant across all the child instances of the DICOM patient whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: patients.getPatientsIdStatistics
tools.description: Get statistics about the given patient

tools.name: patients.getPatientsIdStudies
tools.description: Get detailed information about the child studies of the DICOM patient whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: patients.postPatientsIdAnonymize
tools.description: Start a job that will anonymize all the DICOM instances within the patient whose identifier is provided in the URL. The modified DICOM instances will be stored into a brand new patient, whose Orthanc identifiers will be returned by the job. https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/anonymization.html#anonymization-of-patients-studies-or-series

tools.name: patients.postPatientsIdArchive
tools.description: Create a ZIP archive containing the DICOM patient whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: patients.postPatientsIdAttachmentsNameCompress
tools.description: Change the compression scheme that is used to store an attachment.

tools.name: patients.postPatientsIdAttachmentsNameUncompress
tools.description: Change the compression scheme that is used to store an attachment.

tools.name: patients.postPatientsIdAttachmentsNameVerifyMd5
tools.description: Verify that the attachment is not corrupted, by validating its MD5 hash

tools.name: patients.postPatientsIdMedia
tools.description: Create a DICOMDIR media containing the DICOM patient whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: patients.postPatientsIdModify
tools.description: Start a job that will modify all the DICOM instances within the patient whose identifier is provided in the URL. The modified DICOM instances will be stored into a brand new patient, whose Orthanc identifiers will be returned by the job. https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/anonymization.html#modification-of-studies-or-series

tools.name: patients.postPatientsIdReconstruct
tools.description: Reconstruct the main DICOM tags in DB of the patient whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL. This is useful if child studies/series/instances have inconsistent values for higher-level tags, in order to force Orthanc to use the value from the resource of interest. Beware that this is a time-consuming operation, as all the children DICOM instances will be parsed again, and the Orthanc index will be updated accordingly.

tools.name: patients.putPatientsIdAttachmentsName
tools.description: Attach a file to the given DICOM patient. This call will fail if trying to modify a system attachment (i.e. whose index is < 1024).

tools.name: patients.putPatientsIdMetadataName
tools.description: Set the value of some metadata in the given DICOM patient. This call will fail if trying to modify a system metadata (i.e. whose index is < 1024).

tools.name: patients.putPatientsIdProtected
tools.description: Check out configuration options `MaximumStorageSize` and `MaximumPatientCount`

tools.name: series.deleteSeriesId
tools.description: Delete the DICOM series whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: series.deleteSeriesIdAttachmentsName
tools.description: Delete an attachment associated with the given DICOM series. This call will fail if trying to delete a system attachment (i.e. whose index is < 1024).

tools.name: series.deleteSeriesIdMetadataName
tools.description: Delete some metadata associated with the given DICOM series. This call will fail if trying to delete a system metadata (i.e. whose index is < 1024).

tools.name: series.getSeries
tools.description: List the Orthanc identifiers of all the available DICOM series

tools.name: series.getSeriesId
tools.description: Get detailed information about the DICOM series whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: series.getSeriesIdArchive
tools.description: Synchronously create a ZIP archive containing the DICOM series whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL. This flavor is synchronous, which might *not* be desirable to archive large amount of data, as it might lead to network timeouts. Prefer the asynchronous version using `POST` method.

tools.name: series.getSeriesIdAttachments
tools.description: Get the list of attachments that are associated with the given series

tools.name: series.getSeriesIdAttachmentsNameCompressedData
tools.description: Get the (binary) content of one attachment associated with the given series. The attachment will not be decompressed if `StorageCompression` is `true`.

tools.name: series.getSeriesIdAttachmentsNameCompressedMd5
tools.description: Get the MD5 hash of one attachment associated with the given series, as stored on the disk. This is different from `.../md5` iff `EnableStorage` is `true`.

tools.name: series.getSeriesIdAttachmentsNameCompressedSize
tools.description: Get the size of one attachment associated with the given series, as stored on the disk. This is different from `.../size` iff `EnableStorage` is `true`.

tools.name: series.getSeriesIdAttachmentsNameData
tools.description: Get the (binary) content of one attachment associated with the given series

tools.name: series.getSeriesIdAttachmentsNameInfo
tools.description: Get all the information about the attachment associated with the given series

tools.name: series.getSeriesIdAttachmentsNameIsCompressed
tools.description: Test whether the attachment has been stored as a compressed file on the disk.

tools.name: series.getSeriesIdAttachmentsNameMd5
tools.description: Get the MD5 hash of one attachment associated with the given series

tools.name: series.getSeriesIdAttachmentsNameSize
tools.description: Get the size of one attachment associated with the given series

tools.name: series.getSeriesIdInstances
tools.description: Get detailed information about the child instances of the DICOM series whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: series.getSeriesIdInstancesTags
tools.description: Get the tags of all the child instances of the DICOM series whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: series.getSeriesIdMedia
tools.description: Synchronously create a DICOMDIR media containing the DICOM series whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL. This flavor is synchronous, which might *not* be desirable to archive large amount of data, as it might lead to network timeouts. Prefer the asynchronous version using `POST` method.

tools.name: series.getSeriesIdMetadata
tools.description: Get the list of metadata that are associated with the given series

tools.name: series.getSeriesIdMetadataName
tools.description: Get the value of a metadata that is associated with the given series

tools.name: series.getSeriesIdModule
tools.description: Get the series module of the DICOM series whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: series.getSeriesIdNumpy
tools.description: Decode the given DICOM series, for use with numpy in Python. The numpy array has 4 dimensions: (frame, height, width, color channel).

tools.name: series.getSeriesIdOrderedSlices
tools.description: Sort the instances and frames (slices) of the DICOM series whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL. This URI is essentially used by the Orthanc Web viewer and by the Osimis Web viewer.

tools.name: series.getSeriesIdPatient
tools.description: Get detailed information about the parent patient of the DICOM series whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: series.getSeriesIdSharedTags
tools.description: Extract the DICOM tags whose value is constant across all the child instances of the DICOM series whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: series.getSeriesIdStatistics
tools.description: Get statistics about the given series

tools.name: series.getSeriesIdStudy
tools.description: Get detailed information about the parent study of the DICOM series whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: series.postSeriesIdAnonymize
tools.description: Start a job that will anonymize all the DICOM instances within the series whose identifier is provided in the URL. The modified DICOM instances will be stored into a brand new series, whose Orthanc identifiers will be returned by the job. https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/anonymization.html#anonymization-of-patients-studies-or-series

tools.name: series.postSeriesIdArchive
tools.description: Create a ZIP archive containing the DICOM series whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: series.postSeriesIdAttachmentsNameCompress
tools.description: Change the compression scheme that is used to store an attachment.

tools.name: series.postSeriesIdAttachmentsNameUncompress
tools.description: Change the compression scheme that is used to store an attachment.

tools.name: series.postSeriesIdAttachmentsNameVerifyMd5
tools.description: Verify that the attachment is not corrupted, by validating its MD5 hash

tools.name: series.postSeriesIdMedia
tools.description: Create a DICOMDIR media containing the DICOM series whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: series.postSeriesIdModify
tools.description: Start a job that will modify all the DICOM instances within the series whose identifier is provided in the URL. The modified DICOM instances will be stored into a brand new series, whose Orthanc identifiers will be returned by the job. https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/anonymization.html#modification-of-studies-or-series

tools.name: series.postSeriesIdReconstruct
tools.description: Reconstruct the main DICOM tags in DB of the series whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL. This is useful if child studies/series/instances have inconsistent values for higher-level tags, in order to force Orthanc to use the value from the resource of interest. Beware that this is a time-consuming operation, as all the children DICOM instances will be parsed again, and the Orthanc index will be updated accordingly.

tools.name: series.putSeriesIdAttachmentsName
tools.description: Attach a file to the given DICOM series. This call will fail if trying to modify a system attachment (i.e. whose index is < 1024).

tools.name: series.putSeriesIdMetadataName
tools.description: Set the value of some metadata in the given DICOM series. This call will fail if trying to modify a system metadata (i.e. whose index is < 1024).

tools.name: studies.deleteStudiesId
tools.description: Delete the DICOM study whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: studies.deleteStudiesIdAttachmentsName
tools.description: Delete an attachment associated with the given DICOM study. This call will fail if trying to delete a system attachment (i.e. whose index is < 1024).

tools.name: studies.deleteStudiesIdMetadataName
tools.description: Delete some metadata associated with the given DICOM study. This call will fail if trying to delete a system metadata (i.e. whose index is < 1024).

tools.name: studies.getStudies
tools.description: List the Orthanc identifiers of all the available DICOM studies

tools.name: studies.getStudiesId
tools.description: Get detailed information about the DICOM study whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.description: Synchronously create a ZIP archive containing the DICOM study whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL. This flavor is synchronous, which might *not* be desirable to archive large amount of data, as it might lead to network timeouts. Prefer the asynchronous version using `POST` method.

tools.name: studies.getStudiesIdAttachmentsNameCompressedData
tools.description: Get the (binary) content of one attachment associated with the given study. The attachment will not be decompressed if `StorageCompression` is `true`.

tools.name: studies.getStudiesIdAttachmentsNameCompressedMd5
tools.description: Get the MD5 hash of one attachment associated with the given study, as stored on the disk. This is different from `.../md5` iff `EnableStorage` is `true`.

tools.name: studies.getStudiesIdAttachmentsNameCompressedSize
tools.description: Get the size of one attachment associated with the given study, as stored on the disk. This is different from `.../size` iff `EnableStorage` is `true`.

tools.name: studies.getStudiesIdAttachmentsNameData
tools.description: Get the (binary) content of one attachment associated with the given study

tools.name: studies.getStudiesIdAttachmentsNameInfo
tools.description: Get all the information about the attachment associated with the given study

tools.name: studies.getStudiesIdAttachmentsNameIsCompressed
tools.description: Test whether the attachment has been stored as a compressed file on the disk.

tools.name: studies.getStudiesIdAttachmentsNameMd5
tools.description: Get the MD5 hash of one attachment associated with the given study

tools.name: studies.getStudiesIdAttachmentsNameSize
tools.description: Get the size of one attachment associated with the given study

tools.name: studies.getStudiesIdInstances
tools.description: Get detailed information about the child instances of the DICOM study whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: studies.getStudiesIdInstancesTags
tools.description: Get the tags of all the child instances of the DICOM study whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: studies.getStudiesIdMedia
tools.description: Synchronously create a DICOMDIR media containing the DICOM study whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL. This flavor is synchronous, which might *not* be desirable to archive large amount of data, as it might lead to network timeouts. Prefer the asynchronous version using `POST` method.

tools.name: studies.getStudiesIdMetadata
tools.description: Get the list of metadata that are associated with the given study

tools.name: studies.getStudiesIdMetadataName
tools.description: Get the value of a metadata that is associated with the given study

tools.name: studies.getStudiesIdModule
tools.description: Get the study module of the DICOM study whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: studies.getStudiesIdModulePatient
tools.description: Get the patient module of the DICOM study whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: studies.getStudiesIdPatient
tools.description: Get detailed information about the parent patient of the DICOM study whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: studies.getStudiesIdSeries
tools.description: Get detailed information about the child series of the DICOM study whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: studies.getStudiesIdSharedTags
tools.description: Extract the DICOM tags whose value is constant across all the child instances of the DICOM study whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: studies.getStudiesIdStatistics
tools.description: Get statistics about the given study

tools.name: studies.postStudiesIdAnonymize
tools.description: Start a job that will anonymize all the DICOM instances within the study whose identifier is provided in the URL. The modified DICOM instances will be stored into a brand new study, whose Orthanc identifiers will be returned by the job. https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/anonymization.html#anonymization-of-patients-studies-or-series

tools.name: studies.postStudiesIdArchive
tools.description: Create a ZIP archive containing the DICOM study whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: studies.postStudiesIdAttachmentsNameCompress
tools.description: Change the compression scheme that is used to store an attachment.

tools.name: studies.postStudiesIdAttachmentsNameUncompress
tools.description: Change the compression scheme that is used to store an attachment.

tools.name: studies.postStudiesIdAttachmentsNameVerifyMd5
tools.description: Verify that the attachment is not corrupted, by validating its MD5 hash

tools.name: studies.postStudiesIdMedia
tools.description: Create a DICOMDIR media containing the DICOM study whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: studies.postStudiesIdMerge
tools.description: Start a new job so as to move some DICOM resources into the DICOM study whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL: https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/anonymization.html#merging

tools.name: studies.postStudiesIdModify
tools.description: Start a job that will modify all the DICOM instances within the study whose identifier is provided in the URL. The modified DICOM instances will be stored into a brand new study, whose Orthanc identifiers will be returned by the job. https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/anonymization.html#modification-of-studies-or-series

tools.name: studies.postStudiesIdReconstruct
tools.description: Reconstruct the main DICOM tags in DB of the study whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL. This is useful if child studies/series/instances have inconsistent values for higher-level tags, in order to force Orthanc to use the value from the resource of interest. Beware that this is a time-consuming operation, as all the children DICOM instances will be parsed again, and the Orthanc index will be updated accordingly.

tools.name: studies.postStudiesIdSplit
tools.description: Start a new job so as to split the DICOM study whose Orthanc identifier is provided in the URL, by taking some of its children series or instances out of it and putting them into a brand new study (this new study is created by setting the `StudyInstanceUID` tag to a random identifier): https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/anonymization.html#splitting

tools.name: studies.putStudiesIdAttachmentsName
tools.description: Attach a file to the given DICOM study. This call will fail if trying to modify a system attachment (i.e. whose index is < 1024).

tools.name: studies.putStudiesIdMetadataName
tools.description: Set the value of some metadata in the given DICOM study. This call will fail if trying to modify a system metadata (i.e. whose index is < 1024).

tools.name: system.getPlugins
tools.description: List all the installed plugins

tools.name: system.getPluginsExplorerJs
tools.description: Get the JavaScript extensions that are installed by all the plugins using the `OrthancPluginExtendOrthancExplorer()` function of the plugin SDK. This route is for internal use of Orthanc Explorer.

tools.name: system.getPluginsId
tools.description: Get system information about the plugin whose identifier is provided in the URL

tools.name: system.getStatistics
tools.description: Get statistics related to the database of Orthanc

tools.name: system.getSystem
tools.description: Get system information about Orthanc

tools.name: system.getToolsAcceptedTransferSyntaxes
tools.description: Get the list of UIDs of the DICOM transfer syntaxes that are accepted by Orthanc C-STORE SCP. This corresponds to the configuration options `AcceptedTransferSyntaxes` and `XXXTransferSyntaxAccepted`.

tools.name: system.getToolsDefaultEncoding
tools.description: Get the default encoding that is used by Orthanc if parsing a DICOM instance without the `SpecificCharacterEncoding` tag, or during C-FIND. This corresponds to the configuration option `DefaultEncoding`.

tools.name: system.getToolsDicomConformance
tools.description: Get the DICOM conformance statement of Orthanc

tools.name: system.getToolsGenerateUid
tools.description: Generate a random DICOM identifier

tools.name: system.getToolsMetrics
tools.description: Returns a Boolean specifying whether Prometheus metrics are collected and exposed at `/tools/metrics-prometheus`

tools.name: system.getToolsMetricsPrometheus
tools.description: Get usage metrics of Orthanc in the Prometheus file format (OpenMetrics): https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/advanced-rest.html#instrumentation-with-prometheus

tools.name: system.getToolsNow
tools.description: Get UTC time

tools.name: system.getToolsNowLocal
tools.description: Get local time

tools.name: system.getToolsUnknownSopClassAccepted
tools.description: Shall Orthanc C-STORE SCP accept DICOM instances with an unknown SOP class UID?

tools.name: system.postToolsBulkAnonymize
tools.description: Start a job that will anonymize all the DICOM patients, studies, series or instances whose identifiers are provided in the `Resources` field.

tools.name: system.postToolsBulkContent
tools.description: Get the content all the DICOM patients, studies, series or instances whose identifiers are provided in the `Resources` field, in one single call.

tools.name: system.postToolsBulkDelete
tools.description: Delete all the DICOM patients, studies, series or instances whose identifiers are provided in the `Resources` field.

tools.name: system.postToolsBulkModify
tools.description: Start a job that will modify all the DICOM patients, studies, series or instances whose identifiers are provided in the `Resources` field.

tools.name: system.postToolsCreateArchive
tools.description: Create a ZIP archive containing the DICOM resources (patients, studies, series, or instances) whose Orthanc identifiers are provided in the body

tools.name: system.postToolsCreateDicom
tools.description: Create one DICOM instance, and store it into Orthanc

tools.name: system.postToolsCreateMedia
tools.description: Create a DICOMDIR media containing the DICOM resources (patients, studies, series, or instances) whose Orthanc identifiers are provided in the body

tools.name: system.postToolsCreateMediaExtended
tools.description: Create a DICOMDIR media containing the DICOM resources (patients, studies, series, or instances) whose Orthanc identifiers are provided in the body

tools.name: system.postToolsDicomEcho
tools.description: Trigger C-ECHO SCU command against a DICOM modality described in the POST body, without having to register the modality in some `/modalities/{id}` (new in Orthanc 1.8.1)

tools.name: system.postToolsExecuteScript
tools.description: Execute the provided Lua script by the Orthanc server. This is very insecure for Orthanc servers that are remotely accessible, cf. configuration option `ExecuteLuaEnabled`

tools.name: system.postToolsFind
tools.description: This URI can be used to perform a search on the content of the local Orthanc server, in a way that is similar to querying remote DICOM modalities using C-FIND SCU: https://book.orthanc-server.com/users/rest.html#performing-finds-within-orthanc

tools.name: system.postToolsInvalidateTags
tools.description: Remove all the attachments of the type "DICOM-as-JSON" that are associated will all the DICOM instances stored in Orthanc. These summaries will be automatically re-created on the next access. This is notably useful after changes to the `Dictionary` configuration option. https://book.orthanc-server.com/faq/orthanc-storage.html#storage-area

tools.name: system.postToolsLookup
tools.description: This URI can be used to convert one DICOM identifier to a list of matching Orthanc resources

tools.name: system.postToolsReconstruct
tools.description: Reconstruct the index of all the tags of all the DICOM instances that are stored in Orthanc. This is notably useful after the deletion of resources whose children resources have inconsistent values with their sibling resources. Beware that this is a highly time-consuming operation, as all the DICOM instances will be parsed again, and as all the Orthanc index will be regenerated. If you have a large database to process, it is advised to use the Housekeeper plugin to perform this action resource by resource

tools.name: system.postToolsReset
tools.description: Restart Orthanc

tools.name: system.postToolsShutdown
tools.description: Shutdown Orthanc

tools.name: system.putToolsAcceptedTransferSyntaxes
tools.description: Set the DICOM transfer syntaxes that accepted by Orthanc C-STORE SCP

tools.name: system.putToolsDefaultEncoding
tools.description: Change the default encoding that is used by Orthanc if parsing a DICOM instance without the `SpecificCharacterEncoding` tag, or during C-FIND. This corresponds to the configuration option `DefaultEncoding`.

tools.name: system.putToolsMetrics
tools.description: Enable or disable the collection and publication of metrics at `/tools/metrics-prometheus`

tools.name: system.putToolsUnknownSopClassAccepted
tools.description: Set whether Orthanc C-STORE SCP should accept DICOM instances with an unknown SOP class UID

tools.name: trackingChanges.deleteChanges
tools.description: Clear the full history stored in the changes log

tools.name: trackingChanges.deleteExports
tools.description: Clear the full history stored in the exports log

tools.name: trackingChanges.getChanges
tools.description: Whenever Orthanc receives a new DICOM instance, this event is recorded in the so-called _Changes Log_. This enables remote scripts to react to the arrival of new DICOM resources. A typical application is auto-routing, where an external script waits for a new DICOM instance to arrive into Orthanc, then forward this instance to another modality.

tools.name: trackingChanges.getExports
tools.description: For medical traceability, Orthanc can be configured to store a log of all the resources that have been exported to remote modalities. In auto-routing scenarios, it is important to prevent this log to grow indefinitely as incoming instances are routed. You can either disable this logging by setting the option `LogExportedResources` to `false` in the configuration file, or periodically clear this log by `DELETE`-ing this URI. This route might be removed in future versions of Orthanc.

tools.name: openapi.previewSpec
tools.description: Preview an OpenAPI document before adding it as a source

tools.name: openapi.addSource
tools.description: Add an OpenAPI source and register its operations as tools
